title: How Muslims Preserved One Man's Words for 1400 Years, Perfectly.

sound.ambient: deep space cosmic ambience, low airy hum, vast and clean, subtle shimmer, no music, no melody, no instruments

_note: The isnad system. Ultra-precise: al-Bukhari examined ~600,000 narrations, accepted ~7,000. The warning hadith (Bukhari). No em dashes. Oliver.

segments.text: How do you preserve the exact words of one man, perfectly, across fourteen centuries? Islam built a system the world had never seen.
segments.caption: How were his words preserved?

segments.text: The Prophet Muhammad, peace be upon him, knew his words would be quoted forever. So he gave a chilling warning.
segments.caption: A warning.

segments.arabic: مَنْ كَذَبَ عَلَيَّ مُتَعَمِّدًا فَلْيَتَبَوَّأْ مَقْعَدَهُ مِنَ النَّارِ
segments.translation: Whoever deliberately lies about me, let him take his seat in the Fire.
segments.text: Whoever deliberately lies about me, let him take his seat in the Fire. Reported in Sahih al-Bukhari.
After that, Muslims refused to accept a single saying, unless they could trace exactly who said it, and who they heard it from.
segments.caption: Trace every word.

segments.text: Every hadith carried a chain. This person heard it from that person, who heard it from a companion, who heard it directly from the Prophet.
But a chain is only as strong as its weakest link. So they investigated every narrator in it.
segments.caption: Every link checked.

segments.text: Were they honest? Was their memory exact? Did they ever actually meet the one they quoted? The answers were recorded for hundreds of thousands of people.
An entire science was built, just to catch a single lie.
segments.caption: A science to catch a lie.

segments.text: Imam al-Bukhari examined around six hundred thousand narrations. He accepted only about seven thousand. The rest, he discarded.
Every hadith was graded. Authentic. Good. Weak. Or fabricated. Judged entirely by its chain.
No other text from the ancient world was guarded this strictly. A documented chain, behind every single word.
segments.caption: Unmatched in history.

segments.text: Most of history reaches us as rumor. This reaches us with a receipt.
segments.caption: History with a receipt.

segments.text: Follow for the history they don't teach you.
segments.caption: Follow Ketabi Studio.

segments.text: And our app, Ketabi, is a calm home for your faith. Get it free, at Ketabi Studio dot com.
segments.caption: Download Ketabi.

sources: The science of isnad (chain of narration) and ilm al-rijal (narrator criticism)
Sahih al-Bukhari: the warning against deliberately lying about the Prophet (mutawatir)
Imam al-Bukhari examined ~600,000 narrations and included ~7,000 (with repetition; ~2,600 unique) in his Sahih
